video_title: Haunted Hotel | First 9 Minutes Sneak Peek | Netflix
comment_text:
having watched the show, here are my main opinions:

-the show is neat, the comedy is decent, some of the story lines are interesting, and there are some really neat uses of ghost, demon, and monster pop culture

-its a bit too episodic for my taste, with every episode having a self contained plot and barely any followup between episodes if any
new concepts are introduced and only used again once at most

-the show is not afraid to tackle more taboo themes, such as complex adult relationships, toxic romance, and even self harm(of the deadly kind)

-the characters are all likeable, however, abbadon is the clear winner, as he can be quite funny because of his behavior

-the show does not make good use of its ghosts.
dont get me wrong, they are there, but they are just the backdrop for the rest of the show
there is only TWO non main cast characters that have any sort of relevance in the show, and only one of them is a ghosts
every single other ghost, ghoul, or monster either goes unnamed, is only present for background cameos, or is exclusive to a single episode and never shows up again, which can be dissapointing if you were expecting a show more like GHOSTS (us or uk)

in conclusion: the show is still great fun, but its less of a "family trying to make a haunted hotel work" and more of a "family sitcom where the conflict comes from a supernatural source unique to each episode" not bad, just know what you are going to see least you be dissapointed by the different direction

i will await season 2 eagerly

video_title: Haunted Hotel | First 9 Minutes Sneak Peek | Netflix
comment_text:
Pause on any shot to find thousands of mistakes (the backgrounds were made in procreate using the grid, and are full of lines that don't meet and unfinished shadows and perspective issues that I would expect on any indie project, not on Netflix tho), the characters have no shadows and pop out of the background like a sore thumb (uninspired designs, thick black lines that are all the same size, colours that have nothing to do with the lighting in shots, adult cartoon overdone style, no effort was put into any of them I promise), there isn't a single joke I could find in the whole entire vid, it's Netflix's cliche awful writing (they think that if they say lots of unfunny lines really quickly, it'll trick your brain into thinking it's funny)... what a fun and explorable concept wasted on Netflix's bad writing (like I swear dude either they are hiring anyone to write or paying writers pennies because a fk 4 year old could write funnier or more interesting lines). 
Plot is predictable, characters are shallow (ooh creepy lil girl that looks normal but is cynical and does black magic, wow Netflix I have never seen this in a show before...). No soul, no passion, just another boring "adult" cartoon, it's like they watched the trailer for Gravity Falls and a season of Rick and Morty (post season 3) and made this in 5 minutes. Voice actors are meh, then again not much to work with, like i'd cry if I was casted and my material was: "Did you see a cool bird? Was it a finch?" (riveting). It looks bad. 
Let's stop encouraging this  uninspired cash-grab behaviour when it comes to cartoons especially (but all media fr), let's raise our expectations a bit people, stop consuming slop. There are plenty of amazing animated projects out there that deserve our attention and love. This is not one of them (could be wrong, then again it's a sneak peek not a pilot or concept).
